task_completion.description: Agent opens and reads the scenario briefing file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window showing the contents of 'flood_hazard_scenario.txt'.

task_completion.description: Agent accesses the CAMEO Chemicals website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser open to 'cameochemicals.noaa.gov', showing chemical searches or the reactivity prediction tool.

task_completion.description: Assessment includes the water reaction hazards at Facility Alpha.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The assessment document mentions that Calcium Carbide produces Acetylene, Sodium produces Hydrogen, and/or Phosphorus Trichloride produces Hydrogen Chloride (HCl) gas when reacting with water.

task_completion.description: Assessment identifies secondary cascading hazards at downstream facilities.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The assessment document mentions the creation of Chlorine gas (from HCl + Sodium Hypochlorite) and/or Hydrogen Cyanide (from Sulfuric Acid + Sodium Cyanide).

task_completion.description: Assessment includes ERG guide numbers and large-spill protective action distances.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The assessment document lists ERG Guide 124 for Chlorine (or relevant guides for other worst-case chemicals) and a protective action distance (e.g., 5.0 to 7.0+ miles).

task_completion.description: Assessment provides an overall evacuation recommendation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The assessment document explicitly recommends 'evacuation' of the corridor rather than shelter-in-place.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the completed assessment to the correct file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor title bar, file manager, or terminal showing the file saved as 'flood_cascade_assessment.txt' in the '~/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent actually used the CAMEO Chemicals website to look up information.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the CAMEO Chemicals search or reactivity tool, rather than just guessing the chemical reactions.

integrity.description: The assessment file was actually saved to the disk.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor shows no 'unsaved changes' indicator (like an asterisk next to the filename), or a terminal command confirms the file exists in ~/Documents/.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Facility Alpha water reactions: Calcium Carbide -> Acetylene gas; Sodium -> Hydrogen gas; Phosphorus Trichloride -> Hydrogen Chloride (HCl) gas. Secondary hazards: HCl from Alpha reacts with Sodium Hypochlorite at Beta to produce toxic Chlorine gas (Cl2). At Gamma, Sulfuric Acid + Sodium Cyanide produces lethal Hydrogen Cyanide (HCN) gas. ERG Data: Chlorine is ERG Guide 124, large-spill protective action distance is ~5.0 to 7.0+ miles. Recommendation: EVACUATION is required due to Chlorine and HCN risks. Output file must be saved to ~/Documents/flood_cascade_assessment.txt.
